title: Tool Grinders, Filers, and Sharpeners
description: Perform precision smoothing, sharpening, polishing, or grinding of metal objects.

tasks.text: Monitor machine operations to determine whether adjustments are necessary, stopping machines when problems occur.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inspect, feel, and measure workpieces to ensure that surfaces and dimensions meet specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Study blueprints or layouts of metal workpieces to determine grinding procedures, and to plan machine setups and operational sequences.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select and mount grinding wheels on machines, according to specifications, using hand tools and applying knowledge of abrasives and grinding procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compute numbers, widths, and angles of cutting tools, micrometers, scales, and gauges, and adjust tools to produce specified cuts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Turn valves to direct flow of coolant against cutting wheels and workpieces during grinding.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Set up and operate grinding or polishing machines to grind metal workpieces, such as dies, parts, and tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Dress grinding wheels, according to specifications.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: File or finish surfaces of workpieces, using prescribed hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform basic maintenance, such as cleaning and lubricating machine parts.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Remove finished workpieces from machines and place them in boxes or on racks, setting aside pieces that are defective.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Remove and replace worn or broken machine parts, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Fit parts together in pre-assembly to ensure that dimensions are accurate.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Attach workpieces to grinding machines and form specified sections and repair cracks, using welding or brazing equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Duplicate workpiece contours, using tracer attachments.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect dies to detect defects, assess wear, and verify specifications, using micrometers, steel gauge pins, and loupes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Place workpieces in electroplating solutions or apply pigments to surfaces of workpieces to highlight ridges and grooves.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Straighten workpieces and remove dents, using straightening presses and hammers.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
